body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Scope: text-classifier path, NOT the structured-tool path

`agent-question-pending` is the **trailing-`?` text-classifier** scenario, distinct from `user-blocking-question` even though `scenarios.json` maps both to the same `coverage_id: user-blocking-question`. Per the `scenarios.json` description, this cell "Validates the trailing-? path through the state classifier (session.IsWaitingForUserInput â†’ state_classifier.go decision 2a): at turn end the session transitions workingâ†’waiting instead of workingâ†’ready because the last assistant message ends in `?`. This is the text classifier path, not a structured AskUserQuestion tool." `verify` is `{has_waiting_state: true, final_state: waiting}`; `requires: [headless_mode]`. The recipe prompt is simply: "Reply with exactly one short clarifying question, ending in a question mark."

The existing coverage cell `user-blocking-question/aider = partial/no` ("can ask via /ask interactively but no JSONL-style tool marker; not detected") is the verdict for the **structured AskUserQuestion tool** primitive â€” aider genuinely lacks that. It does NOT describe the trailing-`?` text path this scenario exercises, which is a different mechanism with a different verdict.

## agent_supports: yes

Aider is an LLM-driven REPL. Producing an assistant turn whose final sentence ends in `?` is exactly what the recipe asks for, and aider also ships a first-class `/ask` chat mode (`/ask <msg>`, `/chat-mode ask`, `--chat-mode ask`) for question/clarification turns that make no code edits. There is no structured "question tool" â€” the question is plain prose printed to the terminal and appended to the per-project markdown chat log `.aider.chat.history.md`. For the trailing-`?` primitive (assistant prose ending in `?`), prose support is precisely what's required, so `yes`.

## irrlicht_observes: yes

The aider adapter uses `agent.FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", Parser: RawLineParser{...}}` (agent.go) â€” append-only markdown per project CWD, tailed line-by-line through `ParseLineRaw`. The waiting transition is fully wired:

1. `#### <prompt>` â†’ `user_message` with `ClearToolNames: true`. The tailer clears `lastAssistantText` (tailer.go ~890) and the session goes `working`.
2. Assistant prose (ending in `?`) is buffered between the `####` line and the next `> Tokens:` line (parser.go `ParseLineRaw` prose branch).
3. `> Tokens: â€¦` â†’ `closeModelCall` emits an `assistant_message` whose `AssistantText` carries the buffered prose **with the trailing `?` preserved**. The tailer copies a non-empty `AssistantText` into `lastAssistantText` â†’ `session.LastAssistantText` (tailer.go 886-887; tailer_metrics.go 387). This contract is explicitly pinned by `TestParser_TrailingQuestionMark_PreservedForWaitingClassification` in parser_test.go, which asserts the emitted `assistant_message.AssistantText` ends in `?` precisely "because the tailer feeds assistant_message.AssistantText into LastAssistantText, which session.IsWaitingForUserInput inspects."
4. After ~1500ms quiet, `IdleFlush` synthesizes `turn_done` with an **empty** `AssistantText` (parser.go 216-229). Empty `AssistantText` does NOT overwrite `lastAssistantText`, and the synthetic `turn_done` does NOT set `ClearToolNames`, so the stored question text survives (tailer.go 886-891).
5. On that `turn_done`, `IsAgentDone()` is true, so `ClassifyState` reaches decision 2a. `IsWaitingForUserInput()` â†’ `ExtractQuestionSnippet(LastAssistantText)` finds the trailing `?` (session.go 237-244, 263-286) and returns the `working â†’ waiting` transition (state_classifier.go 47-56). Final state `waiting`, with a visible `waiting` episode â€” matching the scenario's `verify` block.

This is the canonical text-classifier path; the daemon emits the events the spec asserts AS IT EXISTS TODAY. Hence `yes`, not the structured-tool `no`.

## Primitive / capability anchor

Primitive exercised: `tool_calls` + AskUserQuestion (or adapter equivalent). For aider the "adapter equivalent" of pausing-for-input is the trailing-`?` text cue rather than a tool call. The closest capability key in `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` is `tool_calls: true`; aider has no structured question tool (no `AskUserQuestion`/`question` entry), which is why `slash_commands: true` (`/ask`) plus the text-classifier path â€” not a tool marker â€” is what makes this cell observable.

## applicable

yes/yes â†’ `applicable: yes`. Stage 2 (recipe) can proceed; the recording should show `ready â†’ working â†’ waiting` and stop in `waiting`.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant (inverted): aider has NO structured AskUserQuestion/question tool â€” the `partial/no` in the user-blocking-question coverage cell is correct for that structured-tool primitive. This cell passes only because it targets the trailing-`?` TEXT path, a different mechanism. Don't conflate the two.

metric-drift-downstream: the workingâ†’waiting transition is gated on the ~1500ms IdleFlush turn_done (parser.go aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter) AND the daemon's SessionDetector working-refresh tick (~5s). The waiting episode is real but lands a few seconds after the last transcript line, not instantly at end-of-prose.

Detection keys on LastAssistantText, truncated to the last ~200 chars (parser.go truncate). A reply whose `?` falls outside the trailing 200-char window after truncation could be missed; the recipe ('one short clarifying question') keeps it well inside the window.

Heuristic edges: ExtractQuestionSnippet skips rhetorical Q&A pairs and requires the `?` to be followed by whitespace/markdown-wrapper/end-of-string. A plain single-question reply per the recipe is squarely detected (cf. parser_test + session_test cases).

sources.note: Source = FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser} â€” markdown transcript tailed via ParseLineRaw.
closeModelCall emits assistant_message preserving prose (incl. trailing ?); IdleFlush emits turn_done with empty AssistantText; truncate caps at 200 chars.
TestParser_TrailingQuestionMark_PreservedForWaitingClassification pins the contract: assistant_message.AssistantText ends in '?' so session.IsWaitingForUserInput detects it.
Lines ~886-891: lastAssistantText updated only on non-empty AssistantText, cleared only on ClearToolNames â€” so the empty-text idle-flush turn_done preserves the question text.
Decision 2a: IsAgentDone + IsWaitingForUserInput â†’ workingâ†’waiting (the trailing-? text path this scenario validates).
IsWaitingForUserInput â†’ ExtractQuestionSnippet over LastAssistantText (literal trailing '?'); rhetorical/URL exclusions.
agent-question-pending entry: trailing-? text-classifier path, NOT structured AskUserQuestion; verify {has_waiting_state, final_state: waiting}; recipe prompt asks for one trailing-? question.
Existing user-blocking-question/aider cell = partial/no â€” verdict for the STRUCTURED question tool, distinct from this text-path scenario.
tool_calls:true, slash_commands:true, headless_mode:true; no structured question tool flag â€” the /ask + trailing-? text path is what makes this cell observable.
Aider /ask chat mode: ask questions / discuss a plan without editing code; question turns are prose, not a structured tool call.
Confirms .aider.chat.history.md is the per-project markdown chat log the adapter tails.
